evals.prompt: Our Shopify store has been weird — traffic is fine but almost no orders. I haven't looked at any data; I just want to know why we're not getting orders.
evals.expected_output: Despite vague question, full playbook: structured table (drop-off node, hypothesis, A/B copy); checkout UI friction; payment troubleshooting; three-part emails with subject A/B.
expectations: Contains a Markdown table with columns for drop-off node, likely cause (hypothesis), and A/B copy to test
Includes checkout page UI friction section
Includes payment gateway or payment troubleshooting checklist
Includes three-part recovery email sequence (email 1, 2, 3) with subject line variants
Does not refuse due to lack of data — states assumptions where needed

evals.prompt: Checkout conversion is about 1.5%, way below what we used to see. Shipping to Australia is really expensive and a lot of people bounce once they see freight. We have several $200+ SKUs that get added to cart constantly but rarely convert. We use Stripe.
evals.expected_output: Table rows for shipping shock, high-AOV hesitation, payment; AU tactics; Stripe steps; recovery emails.
expectations: Links high shipping to Australia as a primary abandonment hypothesis
Addresses high-AOV add-to-cart without purchase (trust, installments, etc.)
Mentions Stripe-specific troubleshooting (test mode, webhooks, 3DS, logs)
Structured table includes at least shipping-step and payment-related rows
Includes A/B copy suggestions for shipping or recovery

evals.prompt: Can you check if SKU BLUE-MUG is still in stock? Warehouse thinks maybe 12 left.
evals.expected_output: Brief inventory-style response; does NOT dump full abandoned-checkout playbook (should-not-trigger).
expectations: Does not output the full mandatory abandonment table as the main deliverable
Does not force three-part email scripts and full payment troubleshooting for a simple stock question
May briefly answer or ask for system access — appropriate to inventory lookup
